prompt:
Facilities signed off on the C-40 washer controller contract last sprint: types.ts got updated to the new shapes (that file is the contract now, reviewed and frozen — hands off), and whoever did it never touched the rest of the laundromat scheduler. Now the branch won't type-check. CI is `bash run_verify.sh` — npm ci first, node_modules isn't committed — and tsc prints:

  machines.ts(20,27): error TS2339: Property 'durationMin' does not exist on type 'CycleSpec'.
  pricing.ts(17,23): error TS2314: Generic type 'PricingRule<L, R>' requires 2 type argument(s).
  pricing.ts(19,13): error TS7006: Parameter 'load' implicitly has an 'any' type.
  pricing.ts(23,18): error TS2314: Generic type 'PricingRule<L, R>' requires 2 type argument(s).
  pricing.ts(25,13): error TS7006: Parameter 'load' implicitly has an 'any' type.
  receipts.ts(19,34): error TS2322: Type 'string' is not assignable to type 'Temp'.
  schedule.ts(24,12): error TS2554: Expected 2 arguments, but got 1.

Bring the use sites up to the new contract — don't paper over anything with casts; use no `as` type assertions anywhere in the fix. The attendants really do fat-finger the temperature column, and test_washcycle.ts (the acceptance suite, read-only) checks what happens when they do. Same for the runtime handoff to the controllers: the suite drives fake drivers and looks at exactly what they were told. No compiler-option loosening; leave run_verify.sh, tsconfig.json and the package manifests alone.
notes: Declaration-drift triage: types.ts (protected, the post-rollout contract) changed and four use sites across four files are stale, each broken a distinct way — renamed/missing property (machines.ts reads spec.durationMin, TS2339), generic arg count (pricing.ts writes PricingRule<Load> where the interface now takes <L, R>, TS2314 x2 with a cascaded TS7006 implicit-any each since the annotation fails as contextual type), narrowed union (receipts.ts assigns a raw split() string to Temp, TS2322 — the honest fix must add a runtime guard because the tests pin that bad temps throw, so `as Temp` fails the suite), and call arity (schedule.ts calls driver.start with 1 arg, TS2554 — tests pin the fake driver receives (code, minutes)). All 7 diagnostics surface in one tsc 5.9.3 pass (pinned devDependency, npm ci via reference_setup); node --test is the second gate. Toolchain observed: node 26.3.0, tsc 5.9.3.
